programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Cycling: Instructional - Beginner with Caregiver
programs.description: If you are a brand-new or beginner bicycle rider, this is a fun way to gain confidence and experience on a bicycle. Learn basic skills including balance, pedalling, steering and stopping all while remaining on-site. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.Equipment: You must bring your own CSA approved bike helmet and a bicycle with a working bell and a bike lock

programs.name: Pet Portraits in Graphite
programs.description: Learn to create  lifelike pet portraits using graphite in this 4-week workshop. Build  foundational drawing skills, study basic tonal values, and practice techniques  specific to graphite pencil. Working from photo references, develop your  ability to capture personality and expression while gaining an understanding of  how lighting shapes form and detail.

programs.name: Workshop: Natural Dyeing with Wool
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? In this workshop, you will learn how to natural dye with plants and sheep's wool.

programs.name: Examiner Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course is the first step in a three-step process to become certified as an Examiner for the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course prepares candidates to apprentice successfully as an Examiner on the exam of their choice, and it builds on the evaluation experience that candidates have attained in instructor evaluated awards and content.Prerequisite: Current instructor certification for the stream they are seeking examiner certification and being an experienced instructor, having taught or co-taught at least one course in that stream.Equipment: Bring the Award Guide(s) for the streams you wish to become an examiner in.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Group of Seven in One Day
programs.description: Explore the natural beauty of Canada through the lens of the iconic Group of Seven in this one-day painting intensive. Study the techniques, composition and expressive brushwork of these early 20th-century Canadian painters while experimenting with texture, colour and light. Open to all levels, this focused workshop offers an immersive way to deepen your understanding of Canadian landscape painting while developing your own approach.

programs.name: World of Weaving: Beginner Bootcamp
programs.description: Discover the art of weaving and create your own  unique rag rug on our loom!  Learn weaving fundamentals including  setting up a loom, basic stitches, and completing a project - rag rug. Bring  your own fabrics and learn step-by-step how to transform them into colorful,  textured rugs. This hands-on class is perfect for anyone eager to explore the  timeless craft of weaving, Material list will be provided.

programs.name: Cycling: Instructional - Beginner with Caregiver
programs.description: If you are a brand-new or beginner bicycle rider, this is a fun way to gain confidence and experience on a bicycle. Learn basic skills including balance, pedalling, steering and stopping all while remaining on-site. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.Equipment: You must bring your own CSA approved bike helmet and a bicycle with a working bell and a bike lock

programs.name: Workshop: Making music with caregiver
programs.description: Join us for Circle songs, dancing and fun

programs.name: Workshop: Breakfast with the Birds
programs.description: This is a workshop based on the neighborhood birds you don’t want to miss! . You first start the morning with a pancake breakfast and then play themed games and make crafts, this drop-in activity is the perfect choice. Activities take place outside so please be dressed for the weather.

programs.name: Workshop: Mother's Day Mixed Media with Caregiver
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? In this workshop, through guided instruction you will create your very own Mother's Day themed Mixed Media piece. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Outdoor Walk Fit
programs.description: Walking is a great form of exercise for everyone. Combining basic steps with upper body movements, Walk Fit offers both cardio and conditioning benefits..  Meet just outside the front entrance to the Old Fire Hall 30

programs.name: Cycling: Instructional-Beginner
programs.description: If you are a brand-new or beginner bicycle rider, this is a fun way to gain confidence and experience on a bicycle. Learn basic skills including balance, pedalling, steering and stopping all while remaining on-site.Equipment: You must bring your own CSA approved bike helmet and a bicycle with a working bell and a bike lock.

programs.description: Learn how to create a fun character and bring it to life digitally using computer software (Adobe Suite). Take home a high-resolution print of your character and a USB with your completed work in JPEG to send by e-mail or to print again at home.

programs.name: Pool Attendant
programs.description: The Pool Attendant certification is designed to provide lifesavers with the knowledge and skill to supervise wading pools, splashpads, waterslides and amusement devices. In Ontario, a wading pool is less than 0.75 metres (2 feet 6 inches) in depth. Note: City of Toronto wading pool attendant job applicants will be required to have this award if they do not have a Bronze Cross.  Swimming attire is required as this course has an in-water component.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 14 years old and Standard First Aid with CPR C from an approved provider.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.description: Clients who would like to be included on the waiting list for an Allotment in one of the City of Toronto's Allotment Gardens or Greenhouses. This waitlist is for the full bench.

programs.description: Clients who would like to be included on the waiting list for an Allotment in one of the City of Toronto's Allotment Gardens or Greenhouses. This waitlist is for the half bench.

programs.name: Gymnastics: Competitive with Showcase
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on improving strength and form, you will learn and practice skills such as front and back rolls, handstands, cartwheels, bridges, leaps, jumps and turns on a variety of apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Competitive).

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Recital
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Recital
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Club: Explorers Walking and Social
programs.description: Spend time with your peers, get moving and have some fun. Here you will enjoy a guided walk while learning about the neighbourhood.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz with Recital
programs.description: Learn basic jazz positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. This is an 18 week class that ends with a recital.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz with Recital
programs.description: Learn basic jazz positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. This is an 18 week class that ends with a recital.

programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop with Recital
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Dance: Tap with Recital
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of tap dancing such as shuffle, roll, ball tap and brush to help develop and demonstrate a tap routine in a friendly and enjoyable environment.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
